name: ObjRef
summary: Represents a reference to a Rhino object.

constructors.name: ObjRef(Guid)
constructors.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a document.")] public ObjRef(Guid id)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new object reference from a globally unique identifier (Guid).

constructors.name: ObjRef(RhinoObject)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new object reference from a Rhino object.

constructors.name: ObjRef(Guid, ComponentIndex)
constructors.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use version that takes a document.")] public ObjRef(Guid id, ComponentIndex ci)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new object reference from a guid and component index. The component index is used to specify a "piece" of the geometry

constructors.name: ObjRef(RhinoDoc, Guid)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new object reference from a globally unique identifier (Guid).

constructors.name: ObjRef(RhinoObject, PickContext)
constructors.summary: Initialized a new object reference from a Rhino object and pick context

constructors.name: ObjRef(RhinoDoc, Guid, ComponentIndex)
constructors.signature: public ObjRef(RhinoDoc doc, Guid id, ComponentIndex ci)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new object reference from a guid and component index. The component index is used to specify a "piece" of the geometry

methods.name: Brep
methods.summary: Gets the brep if this reference geometry is one.

returns.doc: A boundary representation; or null on error.

methods.name: ClippingPlaneSurface
methods.summary: Gets the clipping plane surface if this reference targeted one.

returns.doc: A clipping plane surface, or null if this reference targeted something else.

methods.name: Curve
methods.summary: Gets the curve if this reference targeted one.

returns.doc: A curve, or null if this reference targeted something else.

methods.name: CurveParameter
methods.summary: If the reference geometry is a curve or edge with a selection point, then this gets the parameter of the selection point.
methods.remarks: If a curve was selected and CurveParameter is called and the SelectionMethod() is not 1 (point pick on object), the curve will be returned and parameter will be set to the start parameter of the picked curve. This can be misleading so it may be necessary to call SelectionMethod() first, before calling CurveParameter to get the desired information.

params.name: parameter
params.doc: The parameter of the selection point.

returns.doc: If the selection point was on a curve or edge, then the curve/edge is returned, otherwise null.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

methods.name: Edge
methods.summary: Gets the edge if this reference geometry is one.

returns.doc: A boundary representation edge; or null on error.

methods.name: Face
methods.summary: If the referenced geometry is a brep face, a brep with one face, or a surface, this returns the brep face.

returns.doc: A boundary representation face; or null on error.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: Geometry
methods.summary: Gets the geometry linked to the object targeted by this reference.

returns.doc: The geometry.

methods.name: Hatch
methods.summary: Gets the hatch if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A hatch; or null if the referenced object is not a hatch

methods.name: InstanceDefinitionPart
methods.summary: If sub-object selection is enabled and a piece of an instance reference is selected, this will return the selected piece.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.ObjRef.InstanceDefinitionPart"]

methods.name: Light
methods.summary: Gets the light if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A light; or null if the referenced object is not a light, or on error.

methods.name: Mesh
methods.summary: Gets the mesh if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A mesh; or null if the referenced object is not a mesh, or on error.

methods.name: Object
methods.summary: Returns the referenced Rhino object.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.ObjRef.Object"]

methods.name: Point
methods.summary: Gets the point if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A point; or null if the referenced object is not a point, or on error.

methods.name: PointCloud
methods.summary: Gets the point cloud if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A point cloud; or null if the referenced object is not a point cloud, or on error.

methods.name: SelectionMethod
methods.summary: Gets the method used to select this object.

returns.doc: The method used to select this object.

methods.name: SelectionPoint
methods.summary: If the object was selected by picking a point on it, then SelectionPoint() returns the point where the selection occurred, otherwise it returns Point3d.Unset.

returns.doc: The point where the selection occurred or Point3d.Unset on failure.

methods.name: SelectionView
methods.summary: If the object was interactively selected in a particular viewport, then SelectionView() returns the view where the object was selected.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.ObjRef.SelectionView"]

methods.name: SelectionViewDetailSerialNumber
methods.summary: If the object was interactively selected in a page space detail view, then SelectionViewDetailSerialNumber() returns the CRhinoObject serial number of the detail view object. Use SelectionView() to get the page view that contains the detail view object. If SelectionViewDetailSerialNumber() returns 0, then the selection did not happen in a detail view.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.ObjRef.SelectionViewDetailSerialNumber"]

methods.name: SetSelectionComponent
methods.summary: When an object is selected by picking a sub-object, SetSelectionComponent may be used to identify the sub-object.

methods.name: SubD
methods.summary: Gets the SubD if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A SubD; or null if the referenced object is not a SubD, or on error.

methods.name: SubDEdge
methods.summary: Gets the SubDEdge if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A SubDEdge; or null if the referenced object is not a SubDEdge, or on error.

methods.name: SubDFace
methods.summary: Gets the SubDFace if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A SubDFace; or null if the referenced object is not a SubDFace, or on error.

methods.name: SubDVertex
methods.summary: Gets the SubDVertex if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A SubDVertex; or null if the referenced object is not a SubDVertex, or on error.

methods.name: Surface
methods.summary: Gets the surface if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A surface; or null if the referenced object is not a surface, or on error.

methods.name: SurfaceParameter
methods.signature: public Surface SurfaceParameter(out double u, out double v)
methods.summary: If the reference geometry is a surface, brep with one face, or surface edge with a selection point, then this gets the surface parameters of the selection point.

params.name: u
params.doc: The U value is assigned to this out parameter during the call.

params.name: v
params.doc: The V value is assigned to this out parameter during the call.

returns.doc: If the selection point was on a surface, then the surface is returned.

methods.name: TextDot
methods.summary: Gets the text dot if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A text dot; or null if the referenced object is not a text dot, or on error.

methods.name: TextEntity
methods.summary: Gets the text entity if the referenced geometry is one.

returns.doc: A text entity; or null if the referenced object is not a text entity, or on error.

methods.name: Trim
methods.summary: If the referenced geometry is an edge of a surface, this returns the associated brep trim.

returns.doc: A boundary representation trim; or null on error

properties.name: Document
properties.summary: Gets the document that owns this object reference.

properties.name: GeometryComponentIndex
properties.summary: Gets the component index of the referenced (sub) geometry. Some objects have sub-objects that are valid pieces of geometry. For example, breps have edges and faces that are valid curves and surfaces. Each sub-object has a component index that is > 0. The parent geometry has a component index = -1.

properties.name: ObjectId
properties.summary: Returns the id of the referenced Rhino object.

properties.name: RuntimeSerialNumber
properties.summary: If > 0, then this is the value of a Rhino object's serial number field. The serial number is used instead of the pointer to prevent crashes in cases when the RhinoObject is deleted but an ObjRef continues to reference the Rhino object. The value of RuntimeSerialNumber is not saved in archives because it generally changes if you save and reload an archive.
